title: Useful Vector Functions
description: Common functions for vectors: sum, mean, length, unique, sort, etc.

slides.title: Summary Statistics 📊
slides.content:
R provides many functions to summarize vectors:

* `sum()` – total of all elements
* `mean()` – arithmetic mean
* `median()` – median
* `min()`, `max()` – minimum and maximum
* `range()` – min and max together
* `var()` – variance
* `sd()` – standard deviation
* `quantile()` – quantiles

These functions return a single number (or two for range).

slides.title: Summary Stats
slides.content: Compute statistics on a numeric vector:
slides.codeSnippet:
x <- c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50)
print(sum(x))
print(mean(x))
print(median(x))
print(min(x))
print(max(x))
print(range(x))
print(sd(x))

quizData.question: What does `range(c(5,2,8,1,9))` return?
quizData.explanation: `range()` returns a vector of length 2: the minimum and maximum.

slides.title: Vector Manipulation Functions 🛠️
slides.content:
* `length()` – number of elements
* `unique()` – remove duplicates
* `sort()` – sort in increasing order
* `rev()` – reverse the vector
* `table()` – frequency table (counts)
* `which()` – indices where logical is TRUE
* `cumsum()`, `cumprod()` – cumulative sums/products

slides.title: Manipulation Examples
slides.codeSnippet:
y <- c(3,1,4,1,5,9,2,6,5)
print(unique(y))
print(sort(y))
print(rev(sort(y)))
print(table(y))
print(which(y > 5))
print(cumsum(y))

quizData.question: Given `v <- c(10, 20, 30, 40)`, what does `which(v > 25)` return?
quizData.explanation: `which()` returns the indices (positions) where the condition is TRUE, so positions 3 and 4.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
Combine functions for powerful one-liners. For example, to get the most frequent element:

```r
names(sort(table(y), decreasing = TRUE))[1]
```

slides.title: Apply Functions
slides.content: Create a vector `scores <- c(88, 92, 75, 88, 96, 75)`. Find the unique scores, sort them descending, and compute the mean of the unique scores.
slides.codeSnippet:
scores <- c(88, 92, 75, 88, 96, 75)
uniq <- unique(scores)
sorted_desc <- sort(uniq, decreasing = TRUE)
print(sorted_desc)
mean_uniq <- mean(uniq)
print(mean_uniq)
